expectations.text: Includes a discovery step (fetch existing param names from DecodingParameters and select the clusterless variant by its naming suffix) before naming one
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 explicitly fetches `DecodingParameters.fetch('decoding_param_name')` and filters with `decoding_param_name LIKE "%clusterless%"`.

expectations.text: Does not invent a specific params name without checking
expectations.evidence: Uses placeholder `<the name you saw above>` and explicitly says 'guessing a name like default_clusterless would just be making something up'.

expectations.text: Does not imply that clusterless and sorted-spikes params live in separate tables — DecodingParameters is a single dj.Lookup; the distinction is by naming convention
expectations.evidence: States 'DecodingParameters is a single dj.Lookup table that holds both clusterless and sorted-spikes parameter sets. There is no separate clusterless decoding parameters table'.
